selectivity_results.code_clause: IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 — 250A Type D MCCB upstream selective against ≤20A Type B/C MCBs at DB-L1 (≥12.5:1 current ratio at L05 worst case; ≥15:1 elsewhere). Curve separation (D upstream vs B/C downstream) + magnetic-pickup time-current separation supports full selectivity. Engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table at 15 kA fault level; fault-level skill to confirm full coordination study.

non_compliance_flags.message: Neutral current 9.8 A is 30.1% of the largest phase (L2 = 32.6 A). Per IEC 60364-1 § 132.6: single-phase load distribution best practice — rebalance recommended. Re-allocate one or more circuits between phases to reduce neutral loading and improve voltage symmetry.

assumptions: IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board (DBO) used for the lighting sub-DB.

IEC 60364-4-41:2018 Clause 411.3.3 universal 30mA Type A RCD applied to all general lighting circuits (L01-L05, L07). Type A required because modern LED drivers contain switched-mode power supplies that produce pulsating DC residual currents.

Emergency lighting L06 supplied on dedicated circuit per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 Clause 560 — no upstream RCD to preserve life-safety availability. FP200 LSZH fire-rated cable specified for the survival period.
External lighting L05 sized at 20A with 4mm² SWA XLPE Cu — handles parking-area floodlight inrush + accommodates the 90m route length with voltage-drop margin.
Estimated PFC at DB-L1 ≈ 15 kA after the 35m 35mm² SWA feeder impedance from 25 kA at MSB-MAIN.
Type C MCBs on L01/L02/L03/L05 chosen for LED driver inrush handling; Type B on lower-power lighting circuits (L04, L07) for tighter earth-fault loop protection.

drawing_notes: DB-L1 located in level-1 electrical riser cupboard.
Fed from MSB-MAIN F01 via 35m 35mm² 5-core SWA XLPE Cu feeder.
L06 emergency lighting circuit cable is FP200 LSZH for fire survival (IEC 60364-5-56 Clause 560).

rationale.chat_summary: DB-L1: 250A TPN+E lighting sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey office, 7 final circuits (3 open-office zones + meeting + external + emergency + decorative), fed from MSB-MAIN F01. 30 mA Type A RCD on general lighting per IEC 60364-4-41 § 411.3.3 (Type A for LED-driver SMPS leakage); L06 emergency routed without RCD on FP200 per IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 + IEC 60598-2-22. Cascade selectivity to F01 verified at 12.5:1 worst-case at the 15 kA PFC.

sections.title: Board Identification
sections.summary: DB-L1 is the dedicated lighting sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey commercial office, located in the level-1 electrical riser cupboard. Built as an IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board (DBO) in an IP4X enclosure (indoor riser location). Role flagged as `lighting_panel` so downstream SLD, riser, and lux-calc skills recognise it as the lighting distribution and route the emergency-lighting subset on the safety-services pathway.

decisions.label: Specialty board role: lighting_panel
decisions.summary: Flagged on the board block so downstream skills route luminaire circuits and identify the emergency-lighting safety-services subset.
decisions.rule: specialty_board role taxonomy — lighting_panel signals the board as the upstream feed for both general and emergency luminaires.

decisions.label: IEC 61439-3 DBO assembly
decisions.summary: IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board class used — IP4X enclosure adequate for the indoor riser cupboard.
decisions.rule: IEC 61439-3 — Distribution boards intended to be operated by ordinary persons (DBO) — assembly verification requirements.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 250A TPN+E intake at 400 V fed from MSB-MAIN F01 via a 35 m 35 mm² 5-core SWA XLPE Cu feeder. Declared PFC at DB-L1 is 15.0 kA — attenuated from the MSB-MAIN bus PFC by the 35 m feeder impedance — covered by 10 kA Icn MCBs through cascade backup from the upstream 250A MCCB (Icu ≥ 25 kA). The 250A rating sized for the aggregate lighting demand (≈19 kW after diversity ≈ 30 A per phase) with significant margin for future zone-expansion and dimmer-driven inrush. Main switch is a 250A 4-pole switch-disconnector per IEC 60947-3 for manual isolation.

decisions.label: 250A intake sized for diversified lighting demand + expansion margin
decisions.summary: 250A rating is well above the ≈30 A per-phase diversified demand — margin handles future zone expansion, dimmer-driven inrush, and LED-driver capacitive transients.
decisions.rule: Lighting feeder sized for steady-state load × inrush factor × growth margin; lighting circuits exhibit significant capacitive inrush from electronic-driver SMPS banks.

decisions.label: PFC 15.0 kA at DB-L1
decisions.summary: PFC attenuated from MSB-MAIN bus PFC by the 35 m 35 mm² feeder impedance; 10 kA Icn MCBs covered via cascade backup from upstream MCCB.
decisions.rule: Cascade backup permits downstream OCPDs with Icn below the prospective fault current when the upstream device interrupts within its rated Icu and the cascade is grounded in the manufacturer table.

decisions.label: Switch-disconnector main per IEC 60947-3
decisions.summary: Non-OCPD 250A 4-pole switch-disconnector at the DB-L1 main — protection is provided by upstream MSB-MAIN F01.
decisions.rule: Isolation per IEC 60947-3 — main switch provides manual isolation without OCPD function, avoiding cascade ambiguity with F01.

sections.title: Circuit Breakdown
sections.summary: 7 circuits: L01/L02/L03 open-office lighting GF/L1/L2 (16A Type C, 2.5 mm²), L04 meeting-rooms (10A Type B), L05 external floodlight at parking + façade (20A Type C, 4 mm² for 90 m route + floodlight inrush), L06 emergency lighting central-battery (10A Type B, 1.5 mm² FP200, NO RCD), L07 decorative (10A Type B). Type C on larger general + external circuits for LED-driver capacitive inrush; Type B on smaller circuits for tighter earth-fault loop. 30 mA Type A RCD on all general circuits — Type A reliably detects pulsating DC from LED-driver SMPS. L06 is the safety-services subset: routed without RCD per IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 + IEC 60598-2-22; cable FP200 LSZH for fire survival.

decisions.label: 30 mA Type A RCD on all general-lighting circuits
decisions.summary: Type A RCD applied to L01-L05 + L07; Type A required because LED-driver SMPS produces pulsating DC residual fault currents that AC-only RCDs do not reliably detect.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 § 411.3.3 additional-protection requirement combined with IEC 60364-5-53 RCD-type selection per load type.

decisions.label: Emergency lighting L06 — NO RCD, FP200 cable
decisions.summary: L06 is the safety-services subset of the lighting distribution. RCD omitted to preserve availability; cable specified FP200 LSZH for fire survival; luminaires per IEC 60598-2-22 emergency luminaire conformity.
decisions.rule: Safety-services lighting must remain energised during the foreseeable fire event — earth-fault auto-disconnection by RCD is incompatible with the availability requirement.

decisions.label: Type C MCB curves on driver-inrush circuits
decisions.summary: L01/L02/L03/L05 use Type C (Ia ≈ 5-10 × In) to ride through LED-driver capacitive inrush; L04/L06/L07 use Type B for tighter earth-fault loop protection where load is lower.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection per IEC 60898-1 — Type C suits capacitive/SMPS inrush; Type B gives tighter Zs requirement for ADS at lower-power circuits.

decisions.label: External lighting L05 sized 20A / 4 mm² for 90 m route
decisions.summary: 20A breaker + 4 mm² SWA XLPE Cu sized to handle parking-area floodlight inrush AND the 90 m route length with voltage-drop margin per IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B.
decisions.rule: Voltage-drop check on long external lighting runs — Annex B 3 % limit for lighting, IEC 60364-5-52 current-rating tables for cable selection.

sections.title: Selectivity Analysis
sections.summary: Two-stage cascade: 250A Type D MCCB at MSB-MAIN F01, non-OCPD 250A switch-disconnector at DB-L1, then final MCBs (10-20A Type B/C). Worst-case ratio is 250:20 = 12.5:1 at L05 — well above the IEC 60898-1 / IEC 60947-2 same-curve / curve-mixed cascade threshold; remaining circuits operate at 15:1+ ratio with comfortable margin. Curve separation (Type D upstream vs Type B/C downstream) further widens the time-current selectivity envelope at the 15 kA fault level. Verdict pass — engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table; fault-level skill to confirm full coordination via the project-graph upstream link.

decisions.label: Worst-case selectivity ratio 12.5:1 at L05
decisions.summary: 250A:20A ratio comfortably meets the IEC 60898-1/IEC 60947-2 cascade threshold with curve separation supporting full selectivity.
decisions.rule: Type D upstream MCCB + Type B/C downstream MCB combination — IEC 60898-1/IEC 60947-2 cascade table confirms full time-current selectivity at the declared fault level.

decisions.label: Engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table
decisions.summary: Selectivity declared by engineer at the 15 kA fault level pending formal coordination study from the fault-level skill. 12.5:1 MCCB-to-MCB cross-curve cascade ratio; lighting circuits fed from F01.
decisions.rule: Engineer declaration is acceptable per IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 when grounded in a manufacturer cascade table; formal IEC 60909-0 + tool calc to be appended at the runtime layer.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: DB-L1 is compliant against IEC 60364-4-41 § 411 (ADS — overcurrent + 30 mA RCD on general lighting circuits), IEC 60364-5-52 (cable selection + voltage drop), IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 (cascade selectivity), IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 (safety services for the L06 emergency lighting subset), IEC 60598-2-22 (emergency luminaire conformity), and IEC 61439-3 (DBO assembly verification). No non-compliance flags. The emergency-lighting circuit L06 is the safety-services subset of an otherwise general lighting board — its omission of RCD is deliberate and grounded in IEC 60364-5-56 § 560.7. Type A RCD selection across general-lighting circuits is the critical electronic-driver-aware choice that AC-only RCDs would fail.

decisions.label: ADS achieved per IEC 60364-4-41 § 411
decisions.summary: Disconnection times met by combination of overcurrent device (MCB) + 30 mA Type A RCD on general circuits; L06 emergency relies on overcurrent only per § 560 safety-services exemption.
decisions.rule: ADS requirements per IEC 60364-4-41 § 411.3 — disconnection in ≤0.4 s for final circuits ≤32 A in TN systems.

decisions.label: Type A RCD justified by electronic-driver leakage profile
decisions.summary: Type A RCDs selected over plain AC type to reliably detect pulsating DC residual currents from LED-driver SMPS — critical for additional-protection function under modern lighting loads.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-5-53 RCD-type selection — Type AC inadequate where loads include rectified or SMPS components.

decisions.label: Emergency lighting L06 governed by safety-services rules
decisions.summary: L06 design and routing governed by IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 + IEC 60598-2-22 (luminaire conformity) + IEC 60331 (cable fire integrity).
decisions.rule: Emergency lighting is a safety service and must satisfy availability + fire-survival requirements distinct from general circuits.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Schedule emitted as 7-row table with 1 spare way (W8) reserved for future lighting expansion. RCD column populated as 30 mA Type A on L01-L05 + L07; explicitly blank on L06 to flag the safety-services exemption clearly to anyone reading the rendered schedule. L06 cable specification annotated FP200 LSZH; remaining cables 3-core SWA XLPE Cu. Voltage-drop on L05 (90 m / 20A / 4 mm²) checked at ≈2.8 % full-load — within the IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B 3 % lighting target. Emergency-lighting central battery autonomy (typically 1-3 h) to be verified by the central-battery commissioning certificate at handover per IEC 60598-2-22 § 22.6.

decisions.label: Spare way W8 reserved for lighting expansion
decisions.summary: 1 spare way left for future-fit (additional zone, occupancy-driven lighting upgrade, or external feature lighting).
decisions.rule: Design margin per IEC 60364-1 Annex C — spare ways reserved at first installation reduce the cost of foreseeable future expansion.

decisions.label: Emergency luminaire commissioning per IEC 60598-2-22
decisions.summary: Central-battery autonomy (1 / 2 / 3 h per AHJ) and luminaire conformity verified at handover; certificate to be appended to the O&M manual.
decisions.rule: IEC 60598-2-22 § 22.6 commissioning + IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 handover documentation requirements.

invariants.evidence: INV-14 board DB-L1 (INT → IEC): R1 jurisdiction→format mapping (INT/EU → IEC 60364-5-51 §514) holds; R2 board_label.text+svg populated, no {{}} remnants; R3 board_label.text matches IEC format regex; R4 7/7 circuits carry circuit_label.text+svg, no {{}} remnants; R5 every circuit_label.text matches IEC circuit regex; R6 tool_call_pending booleans set on all labels.

INV-15 board DB-L1 (INT (IEC)): all 7 circuits carry diversity_basis. load_types: fixed_resistive×1, lighting_continuous×6 — all valid enum (Rule 1 PASS). factors: 1.0×7. Citations follow IEC 60364-3 §132 / IEC 60364-5-55 §552 family — all ≥20 chars with recognisable clause markers (Reg / § / OSG / Table / CoP / TM) (Rule 2 PASS). Rule 3 N/A (no lift or ev_charger circuits on this board). Rule 4 N/A (no largest_plus_remainder_pct circuits — Rule 4 vacuously holds).
